park_name: Laura (SA) Community Caravan Park, Southern Flinders Ranges

reviews.review_text: Great little park, clean and friendly

We have stayed here several times and has been neat and tidy. Unfortunately the park is starting to look a bit unloved.Garden beds neglected,our site was untidy. Plenty of room and put right out of the way. Scines were good. Bit disappointed

Beautiful park with great amenities- clean and well cared for.  Lovely welcoming and very helpful team|Camp kitchen well stocked and everything in working order .Beautiful pitches - with lots of privacy - site in great location for exploring Laura - town is really interesting and very friendly
Nice clean park, very well equipped communal kitchen, friendly and helpful staff, who great you with lovely freshly baked scones with cream (yummy!)
Beautiful little caravan park staff super nice and the scones on arrival are a nice touch
The best tent site that we have come across in SA. Grass, new facilities close to the site, clean and quiet. We stayed extra nights it was so good.
Always a great place to stay, quiet, clean & only a short walk to everything in town.

What a hidden gem! The most beautiful little park with super friendly owners. They're honestly going above and beyond for a great guest experience! We were welcomed with two yummy scones, super clean amenities and such a well kept park. They even provide bath mats!
There are sunny and shaded sites, plenty of space, great rates and it's all walking distance into town. Would stay again in a heartbeat!

We loved our stay at Laura caravan park. The grassy site was perfect and the little touches such as the welcome scones and the bathmats that were provided in the spotless amenities block were much appreciated. The team at the park were helpful and friendly and we will definitely be returning for another stay.

We loved our stay at Laura community park. We were greeted with scones and cream on arrival and Melissa was super friendly. There were bins close by and the amenities building was kept very clean. We would definitely return to this quaint little town.

What a beautiful little caravan park, right from the start with being offered scones, jam and cream on arrival!! All the facilities are clean and well thought out, there are even clothes baskets in the laundry for guests to use. If you want a secluded site ask for site 18, very private and quiet. And make sure you visit Little Blessings Brewery across the road, such lovely people as well. Thanks for such a wonderful stay 😃

Friendly and welcoming staff and the park is well maintained but also feel there overcharging for unpowered sites. I am completely self contained didnt use any of their facilities. The place where we were set up there was heaps of  sharp prickles and massive ants nests, we have small children so wasnt safe to play also there was plenty of recycling bins but no rubbish bins, I had to walk to the other side of the park. This park definitely has potential just poor layout on somethings and definitely over charging
Great caravan Park
Camped under the stars on a beautiful summers night in my double king daddy swag solo pure bliss 👌😴❤️
Clean toilets and showers. We had a lovely stay and got scones when we checked in. These were delicious by the way
Laura Caravan Park great spot, all amenities very clean and tidy, and Melissa and Tristan friendly and welcoming, keep up the good work.          Highly recommend
This caravan park is a great place to stay, caretakers are brilliant couple ,
Travelling in a 23ft6 Majestic Knight caravan. Really enjoyed the community,  close to everything. A Quiet spot very relaxing.

Some of the staff must have been having a bad day - actually a bit frustrated trying to help guest check in and take calls. We wanted to stay an extra night but that experience found us staying just the one night.

Great location and lovely park with plenty of options for either grass or gravel sites. Clean amenities. Staff very friendly and was greeted on arrival with fresh scones, jam and cream. Much appreciated and needless to say  partner devoured as soon as van was set up! Highly recommend.

This is a lovely caravan park!  One of the best we've stayed in.  The welcome was friendly, the scones were delicious and a lovely touch.  ||The care taken in maintaining the caravan park is obvious and this is only the second time I have encountered fresh, clean bath mats for the use of campers in the amenities block...the other was in Waikerie...and I have stayed in 100's of caravan parks and camping grounds over the course of a Lap of Australia and many other shorter trips.  This alone, would see me coming back!  ||The sites are green and it's a very easy walk into the lovely town of Laura.

Amazing! We stayed in a field unpowered campsite & loved our experience. Service/ manager was warm and friendly & amenities were clean and modern and within feet from our site. The park had little areas to explore everywhere such as a forest walk through historic machinery & nature & a vibey little rustic camp kitchen that had a book library, kitchen, fire place etc. The park even had a bike repair station!
Pricing was $28 for our unpowered site which we feel is well worth it for the experience & amenities.
The location is within short walking distance to town & across the road from the brewery. This caravan park is so full of character & would be one our favourite campsites whilst on the road so far!
Beautiful Caravan park, great, clean amenities, friendly service, great camp kitchen and lovely gardens.

Very nice park
Love the free scones on arrival
Managers are super nice people

Absolutely loved this park. The amenities so clean. The park so neat and so many little art work everywhere. Staff were first rate. The scones,  jam, and cream on arrival were a real treat. Can't fault this place at all. Loved it!!

Great little park, large site, beautiful layout with flower bed dividers. Clean amenities & great camp kitchen. Loved the homely touch with the scones Thanks Mel. They couldn't do enough for us. Highly recommend. Even opposite a great little brewery for those who enjoy a little tipple.

Wonderful community run park, staff welcoming and very friendly.  Great touch of a couple of lovely fresh scones with jam and cream.
Well done, lovely sites, clean amenities and even complimentary bath mats for the showers.
Worth the stop.
Lovely town and people.
Fabulous community owned caravan park. Friendly staff. The cabins are spacious, clean, functional and reasonably priced. Very easy walk to all the main attractions in Laura from here.
Great park, set up for bike tourists and in a perfect location on the Mawson trail. The free scones on arrival was a pleasant surprise. Thank you!

Most Mawson Trail bike friendly campground so far. Great facilities and unpowered sites aren’t just an afterthought like many other places.
Will definitely be back one day. :-)

Just home from a wonderful 4 days at Laura caravan park. Managers take great pride in the park and ever so helpful. From getting your van on-site, fantastic scones and cream on arrival to spotless amenities. The camp kitchen has absolutely everything you need and a lot more as well. So quiet despite being full and the big bonus a 5 minute walk to the pub and shops. Will stay again. Thanks guys lust loved it.

Extremely helpful, called up last minute to get a couple of spots, staff were great welcoming and helpful.

Location, facilities and sites are 10/10 highly recommend it.

A very nice caravan park close to everything in town. Good sites on lawn or handstand. Shower blocks are very clean and tidy . Managers are very friendly. Quaint practice of giving new arrivals fresh scones with jam and cream.
Laura is a very nice quiet little town in an attractive rural part of SA.
Will definitely stay here again. 😊

Scones on arrival. Bath mats provided.
Beautiful clean amenities.Great camp kitchen with pizza oven.
So clean and gardens immaculate

Clean.
Complimentary scones on arrival and a Brewery across the road!!
🌟Lovely to be back...great placeto be in the world...thankyou...all the way from WA...Robyn and Brett Asphar 💛

We were met by Melissa with a lovely smile and complimentary scones, jam and cream on our arrival. We stayed in cabin 3 . Immediately we were impressed with how clean and spacious the cabin was. The towels were luxurious and the bed comfortable.  The gas stove was spotless as were the fridge and microwave. Overall we could not recommend highly enough the cabins at Laura Caravan Park.
Great park, clean amenities, very good camp kitchen, even with pizza oven

Fantastic little caravan park. Complimentary scones on arrival, a playground and a brewery across the road, this park is an absolute hidden gem. If you're looking for an overnighter or a weekend away or somewhere to pull up stumps for a few nights you can't go wrong.

Such a lovely caravan park, not too big, well looked after, good amenities, quiet, lovely owners and lots of trees and a rustic garden out the back. We celebrated a special birthday here in 2 cabins with our family. Laura is a great little town and the caravan park the perfect place to stay.
What a great park friendly hosts and really nice atmosphere, right in town. Amenities are great with a good camp kitchen to boot make a point and stay.

Stayed in a well-equipped Cabin it had everything including a comfy bed and the best towels ever! Fresh scones on arrival was a nice surprise, and they were yummy. The Caravan Park was quiet even though was busy with a lot of caravans, and close to the main street and shops. We will be back in the near future!|Laura has all of the things I love, a brewery, chocolate shop and Golden North Ice Cream which was available in most shops.

Great park
There is plenty of room facilities spotless.

Fantastic little caravan Park.
Scones jam and cream on arrival.
Well done to the managers of this gem

Lovely welcome (scones and jam and cream) beautifully clean and spacious ( we’re next to a pepper corn tree and it smells divine) however we are close to the main north road (Horrocks highway) noisy traffic at all hours!

We found the staff were quite abrupt and lacked that welcoming vibe. I found this on the phone prior and on arrival. We got there early as we had friends there in a caravan. We noticed our cabin had been cleaned and ready so my husband went to see if we could check in 1.5 hours early. That didn't go down well. It's all about how you talk to your customers and in this business you can't afford to be rude. A polite no you have to wait until 2pm is all we needed. You can't defend this type of behavior towards customers as I've seen in a response to another similar review. We've actually had other parks call us to tell us our cabin is ready if we were wanting to check in early so it's not the norm across all parks. In this current climate you can't afford to be so totally unwelcoming to your guests. People skills or lack of really kill businesses.

Our cabin was very clean and spacious plus the towels were lovely. Many things in our cabin needed maintenance or just lacked that routune check. Some things were dangerous like the screen door locking you in and the kettle not switching itself off. The controller for the A/C was faulty so at times we couldn't use the heating. We didn't feel like we could go tell the owners as they weren't approachable so I told them via their feedback email. I was disappointed as I have been wanting to try this park for quite some time. The scones on arrival suggested that we were in for a different customer experience than what we had. Speaking to other guests during our stay this was the common theme and not just our experience. We wouldn't go back.
A lovely spot to park up for the night or longer. The scones with cream and jam was a fabulous touch on check-in with lovely spacious clean amenities. 👌

Awesome little caravan park and great value. Amenities are great. Scones on arrival WOW!!Laura is a great place for a stopover for a day or two. Make sure you check out the brewery (conveniently across road). And definitely go to CocoLaura and try Golden North ice cream.
Quiet and welcoming
Great place to put your caravan .. amenities were v v clean .

What a fantastic park ! A credit to the community/management. Lots of shade wonderfully clean ammenities, a friendly welcome and not to mention the delicious plate of scones jam and cream . Highly recommend this park !!
Clean and quiet caravan park.

What a beautiful caravan park.
We stayed in our caravan. The bays are big.
The ablutions are a little old but very clean & well maintained.
The camp kitchen is also lovely & clean too.
The staff are lovely.
Complimentary fresh scones jam & cream on arrival was a surprise & yummy too.
Thank you for the enjoyable stay.

Camping in the van. Concrete slabs on some sites. Plenty room. Very shady. Camp kitchen layout is very good. Amenities are good. Scones on arrival. Nice one. Mirco brewery across the road. Plenty walking trails. TV reception good. Internet good. Close to town centre.
Own caravan. Nice clean amenities and lovely little park.  Enjoyable. Very helpful staff. Laura a nice relacing place to walk around.

Great sites with concrete pad, ample room to park car and nice shade trees. Camp kitchen great meeting place and well equipped. Amenities were spotless and a new block is under construction.|Walking distance to cafe, hotel, IGA and butcher. Easy drive to Pt Pirie, Gladstone, Booleroo and beyond.

C'est un super parking.  Belle place pour notre van.
Sanitaire, cuisine = d'une propreté incroyable

Nous avons traversé l'australe pendant 1 mois. C'est le camping le plus agréable, le plus accueillant, le plus propre, au meilleur prix que nous ayons fait. je recommande très très vivement ce camping. bravo et merci encore pour votre accueil.
Great little park. Very neat and tidy.
Fantastic place
Great camp site amenities good

We were in our own caravan &  parked next to the outdoor fireplace.
People were drawn to the fire like moths to a light.
Had a fantastic evening meeting other travellers & sharing stories.
Everyone loved the Laura park & facilities....especially the scones, jam & cream you get on arrival.
Brewery directly across the road for a few drinks & and the
meal was amazing.

We especially enjoyed our welcome of the scones with jam and cream, something unique to the park not experienced anywhere else.|We had a a lovely spacious grass site, which suited us perfectly.|The layout of the park is terrific and we appreciated the opportunity to choose our own site from the several ones available for our stay.|The amenities were very clean at all times and we appreciated the bath-mats provided for the showers.|The cleanliness and maintenance of the park was exemplary.
Still the complimentary jam and cream scones on arrival,a great base camp for the area.
Drive through powered site amazing,  such a lovely country town and the scones on arrival was a treat.

The best 'small town' caravan park in our
50 years of caravanning - 7 stars!!!

We always make a point of stopping here on our way back from an outback trip. I thought it couldn't get any better than the scones, amenities,  friendliness, ambience, price, but now there are fresh bath Mats to use after your shower. 👏👏
They bought me scones to my caravan that where charred black and the tea they served was cold as they service they provide
Excellent very clean caravan park, beautiful amenities and a lovely set up. Lovely scones. Well price . Highly recommended

Fantastic little caravan park, close walk to playground and main street of the town. We stayed for 2 nights, spacious sites, great camp kitchen area with fire pits, even supplied wood and scones on arrival for free! We will definitely be staying again.

On arrival you get a warm scone with jam and cream for each person staying. Had one of the best camp kitchens that I have seen. Could burn firewood if you liked with a donation jar out if you did! They even provided you with a shower floor towel. One of the overall nicest parks we have stayed in.

Simon and Naomi are one of the friendliest, helpful, and kind care takers I have met on my travels.
They truly do go above and beyond and clearly take great pride in the management of the park.
Thank you for a lovely stay, I may be back another day ✨

Loved this unique stunning park. Came across the park by default and the managers Simon and Naomi were most accommodating. During our stay here we had some tragic news which required a return trip us to Perth. Simon and Naomi were so very accommodating and we had no reservations about leaving the van parked up until our return.

The park is very clean and well kept. The  managers are really good at what they do.
We had only been at the park for about an hour when the managers bought us a lovely plate of scones, jam and cream for afternoon tea which was cooked by Naomi - hot straight out the oven. What a wonderful gesture and the scones were just beautiful. Such a lovely welcome that adds to the awesome experience at staying at this gorgeous little park.

We would rate this caravan park highly and would recommend it to anybody looking for great country hospitality. Definitely 5 +
Rooms: 5/5  |  Service:
One of our favourite caravan parks. Have stayed here a couple of times and always great!
I love this CP I have used it on now on 3 occasions now, it never disappoints. And don't forget the fresh scones, jam and cream 😀

A cute quiet park with lovely managers, Naomi and Simon, with Devonshire scones on arrival a very nice touch.
Super clean amenities and an awesome fully equipped camp kitchen.
Park is ideally located, with an easy walk to the IGA, cafe, pub and the Little Blessing Brewery just across the street.
A great base camp to explore the Southern Flinders.
Loved our stay and definitely recommend!
Comfy beds with electric blankets  comfy pillows  easy walk to street and also nice and quiet  clean cabins  worth a stay
A great little park, lovely managers and the scones where beautiful.
Top community caravan park! Great facilities and position. The scones and cream are to die for.💕
A great base to stay whilst exploring the Southern Flinders region.
We arrived too late for the scones, but really enjoyed the night. Fresh bathmats in the shower was a real highlight.

We loved this little park, so green and quiet. Great service, including scones, jam and cream hand delivered by the owner not long after we set up. Imagine that! There were even clean bath mats in a basket by the door of the bathroom.

The Stone Hut pies are superb and so is Golden North ice cream! The lady at the Laura Information Centre was really helpful and we enjoyed one of the walking routes around the pretty town.

Just leaving this gorgeous little caravan park now and I must say it’s    Horeal little gem in the lovely town of Laura South Australia.
The park is small but very clean and well kept.  We had a great site which was a group of 4 that had little gardens in between to give you a little sense of privacy.
We had only been at the park for about an hr when one of the managers bought us a lovely plate of scones, jam and cream for afternoon tea which he said had” been made by his wife and are hot straight out the oven. What a wonderful gesture and the scones were just beautiful.  We noticed the man went to a lot of the sites doing the same thing. Such a lovely extra that adds to the awesome experience at staying at this gorgeous little park.
We only stayed the one night( we are on our way to port Lincoln) but we want to come back here real soon to really check out what seems to be like a beautiful country town of Laura. It’s the home of Golden north icecream and has a chocolate store that is the only handmade chocolate products in Australia containing Pectin( fun fact)

All in All though we rate this caravan park highly and would recommend it to anybody looking for great country hospitality. Definitely 5 stars from us.
Open and friendly

What a beautiful park
Scones cream and jam on arrival 😋
Very well looked after  and great management.
Will definitely stay again .
Wendy and darren Smith
Great unpowered camping, everything clean and welcome scones on arrival was so nice!

Very Welcoming
Clean and tidy
Great facilities
Took our own caravan

My favourite caravan park we have stayed at on our trip from WA. Complimentary scones on arrival, complimentary floor mats for the shower, epic camp kitchen and great atmosphere. Close walk to good country pub dinner and local shops. Very clean place to stay and an overall lovely atmosphere. Caravan park managers were extremely accommodating, friendly and present around the park. Highly reccommend.
Nice park, camp kitchen also has a fire inside, love the scones, bathmats provided for showers, gardens out back well maintained. Easy walk into town

Great little Caravan Park, has drive through sites, several cabins after set up some hot scones jam and cream were delivered to our van, camp kitchen has a pot belly but is open on one side.
We didn't stay in the cabins.

This is a lovely friendly park. Everyone was so helpful to us when we arrived on Friday with a Vehicle problem. Our Van was put on-site while we waited for the RAA. We had arranged to stay 3 nights in Laura this time, having only stayed 1 night previously.
Sadly this will probably be our last time in Laura as travelling with the van is becoming hard.
We wish everyone in Laura well in the future. 😊😍
Excellent caravan park ... a credit to the managers

What a delightful find this park was. Sites are spacious, both grassed, with pad or drive through gravel. Amenities old but clean, well serviced. Naomi & Simon are lovely & everyone is welcomed with freshly made scones jam & cream on arrival. The camp kitchen has everything. Who could ask for more.
Lovely big cabin. Beautiful  hot scones  jam & Cream on arrival. Great area to visit
Good people. Spotless park. Underrated location.
2nd return and didn't disappoint , scones as yummy as ever. Getting busier now the word has got around. Pays to book.

Looking for a weekend away we decided to head up Laura way, and given reasonable cost of Laura Community Caravan Park decided to stay.  What a great park, well laid out, clean facilities, excellent service, and a complimentary serve of scones (that's enough for us to come back).  Does not have all the trimmings of those other big brand parks, which makes this so much better, and priced appropriately.  Highly recommend this park.
